questions.question: Which logic gate outputs HIGH (1) only when its inputs are different?
In the typical digital IC design flow, which major stage involves converting the Register Transfer Level (RTL) description into a gate-level netlist using standard cells from a library?

In a multi-core processor system, what mechanism is primarily responsible for ensuring that all processors see a consistent view of data stored in shared memory, particularly when multiple caches might hold copies of the same data block?

Which reliability degradation mechanism in CMOS devices is primarily caused by prolonged stress under negative gate-to-source voltage and elevated temperatures, leading to an increase in threshold voltage and reduction in drain current over time, especially significant for p-type MOSFETs?

Within the Central Processing Unit (CPU), which component is responsible for interpreting instructions, coordinating the flow of data, and generating control signals to other components like the ALU, registers, and memory?
